title: ArCharacterArcRelation
description: A13 (aristotelian-sketch-03). Structural relation between two or more ArCharacter records *within a single ArMythos*. Hamlet's three-way tragic-hero parallelism (pairwise mirror Hamlet-Laertes + foil Hamlet-Claudius) is the canonical case; Lear's instrumental relations (Edmund→Gloucester + Edgar→Gloucester) are the first multi-record, multi-kind set in the corpus. Distinguished from A10 ArMythosRelation by scope: A10 ties ≥2 ArMythos records (inter-mythos); A13 ties ≥2 ArCharacter ids within one ArMythos named by mythos_id. Encoding-scope record (tupled as AR_*_CHARACTER_ARC_RELATIONS at module level); passed to verify() via the character_arc_relations kwarg. Sketch-05 (A17) adds directionality + polarity as orthogonal fields; canonical kinds are symmetric by construction, non-canonical kinds (e.g., 'instrumental') are typically directional and carry polarity. PFS16-CAR1..CAR8.

id.description: Stable identifier for this relation (e.g., 'ar_hamlet_laertes_mirror'). PFS16-CAR1.

kind.description: Open non-empty string. Canonical vocabulary {parallel, mirror, foil} per A13; A7.10 check 1 emits severity=noted for non-canonical values (e.g., 'instrumental'). Schema admits open non-empty string without close-enum, mirroring A10 / PFS13-MR2's canonical-plus-open discipline. PFS16-CAR2.

character_ref_ids.description: Participating ArCharacter ids within mythos_id's characters tuple. ≥2 required (A7.10 check 2 separately enforces resolution within the named mythos at verification time). Order is load-bearing when directionality='directional' — position 0 is wielder, position 1 is target (A17, sketch-05). Plain-string references per PFS6-X2. PFS16-CAR3.

mythos_id.description: The ArMythos id this relation binds to. A7.10 check 2 enforces mythos resolution at verification time. Plain-string reference per PFS6-X2. PFS16-CAR4.

over_event_ids.description: Optional substrate event ids where the arcs track / mirror — substrate-check skipped when not threaded. No minItems; empty array is the Python default. Plain-string references per PFS6-X2. PFS16-CAR5.

annotation.description: Optional author prose annotation. Python default is empty string; schema admits the empty string and absence interchangeably. PFS16-CAR6.

directionality.description: A17 (sketch-05). Canonical-plus-open vocabulary {symmetric, directional} + empty string (default; derives from kind — canonical → symmetric, non-canonical → empty / no claim). When 'directional', character_ref_ids position 0 is wielder, position 1 is target. A7.15 enforces value-canonicity and kind-compatibility. PFS16-CAR7.

polarity.description: A17 (sketch-05). Canonical-plus-open vocabulary {malicious, therapeutic, neutral, sanctioned} + empty string (default; not-applicable). Strongest signal on directional non-canonical relations. A7.15 emits noted on polarity-set-but-directionality-symmetric; paired-non-canonical-polarity-contrast noted when two records share target + non-canonical kind + different polarities (A7.15 check 5, the corpus's first highlight observation). PFS16-CAR8.
